questions.text: Who is the author of 'Madam Rides the Bus'?
questions.explanation: Vallikkannan is the author of this story, originally written in Tamil.

questions.text: What was the real name of Valli?
questions.explanation: Her full name was Valliammai, but she was called Valli for short.

questions.text: How old was Valli?
questions.explanation: Valli was an eight-year-old girl.

questions.text: What was Valli's favorite pastime?
options: Standing at the front doorway of her house
questions.explanation: She had no playmates, so she enjoyed watching the happenings in the street.

questions.text: What was Valli's overwhelming desire?
questions.explanation: Her deepest wish was to ride the bus that traveled between her village and the nearest town.

questions.text: What did Valli carefully listen to in order to plan her trip?
options: Conversations between neighbors and regular bus users
questions.explanation: She gathered details by listening to regular passengers and asking discrete questions.

questions.text: How far was the town from Valli's village?
questions.explanation: The distance between the village and the town was six miles.

questions.text: What was the one-way fare for the bus trip?
questions.explanation: The fare was thirty paise for one way.

questions.text: How much time did the bus take to reach the town?
questions.explanation: The bus trip to the town took forty-five minutes.

questions.text: Valli saved money by sacrificing which of these?
questions.explanation: She resisted every temptation to spend money so she could save sixty paise.

questions.text: Which time did Valli choose for her bus ride?
questions.explanation: She chose the afternoon while her mother was napping.

questions.text: The bus conductor was a person of what nature?
questions.explanation: The conductor was a cheerful person who called Valli 'Madam' to joke around.

questions.text: Why did Valli stand up on her seat?
options: A canvas blind blocked her view
questions.explanation: The lower part of the window was covered, so she stood up to see over it.

questions.text: Who told Valli, 'You shouldn't stand like that... Sit down'?
questions.explanation: An elderly man in the bus was concerned for her safety.

questions.text: Valli found the elderly woman who sat beside her 'absolutely repulsive' because:
options: She had big holes in her ears and ugly earrings
questions.explanation: Valli disliked her large earrings and the sight of her betel juice.

questions.text: What animal did Valli see running in the middle of the road?
questions.explanation: A young cow was running right in front of the bus, which Valli found very funny.

questions.text: What did the bus driver do when the cow was in front of the bus?
questions.explanation: He sounded his horn repeatedly to clear the way.

questions.text: Why did Valli laugh until there were tears in her eyes?
options: The sight of the galloping cow
questions.explanation: The sight of the cow's tail high in the air as it ran was hilarious to her.

questions.text: What did Valli do when the bus reached the town?
questions.explanation: She didn't get off because she only had enough money for the return fare.

questions.text: The conductor offered Valli a cold drink because:
options: It was part of the ticket
He wanted to be kind to the 'madam'
questions.explanation: The conductor was impressed by her independence and offered to buy her a treat.

questions.text: What saddened Valli on her return journey?
options: The sight of the dead cow
questions.explanation: She saw the same cow she had laughed at earlier, now lying dead by the roadside.

questions.text: The dead cow made Valli feel:
options: Haunted by the memory and lost her enthusiasm
questions.explanation: The sight of death dampened her spirits for the rest of the trip.

questions.text: What time did Valli get back to her village?
questions.explanation: The bus returned to the village at about three-forty.

questions.text: Who was Valli's mother talking to when Valli returned?
questions.explanation: She was talking to an aunt from South Street.

questions.text: How did Valli's aunt describe her?
options: A 'chit of a girl' who pokes her nose into others' business
questions.explanation: The aunt thought Valli acted too much like a grown-up.

questions.text: Valli's mother said, 'So many things... without our knowledge.' Valli smiled because:
options: She was happy to see her mother
She had just returned from a secret trip
questions.explanation: She was amused because her mother didn't know about her bus ride.

questions.text: The theme of the story relates to:
options: A child's curiosity and first encounter with death
questions.explanation: It explores Valli's desire to see the world and her realization of the fragility of life.

questions.text: What does the word 'discreet' mean in the context of Valli's questions?
questions.explanation: She asked questions carefully so as not to reveal her plan.

questions.text: Why didn't Valli get off at the town to see the sights?
questions.explanation: Valli was cautious, budget-conscious, and needed to return before her mother woke up.

questions.text: The word 'kindred' in the story refers to:
questions.explanation: Kindred refers to being of a similar nature or character.

questions.text: How much money did Valli have for the return trip?
questions.explanation: She saved 60 paise total: 30 for going and 30 for returning.

questions.text: Valli's behavior on the bus showed she was:
questions.explanation: She refused help and insisted on paying her own way, showing great confidence.

questions.text: The phrase 'wistfully' is used to describe Valli looking at:
questions.explanation: She looked wistfully (with longing) at people getting on and off the bus.

questions.text: Which of these features did Valli NOT observe from the bus?
questions.explanation: She saw landscape and fields, but there is no mention of an ocean.

questions.text: Valli's planning shows she was a child of:
questions.explanation: Her careful saving and timing demonstrate a mature and calculating mind.

questions.text: The conductor called Valli 'Madam' as a sign of:
questions.explanation: He was joking because of the way she was acting like a grown-up.

questions.text: What was the 'deadly secret' Valli kept?
questions.explanation: Her trip to the town and back was a secret from her mother.

questions.text: Valli's encounter with the dead cow represents:
questions.explanation: It's her first real encounter with the harsh reality of death.

questions.text: In the story, Valli is depicted as:
questions.explanation: She doesn't just watch the bus; she plans and executes a ride.

questions.text: The story is primarily set in:
options: A small village and a bus route
questions.explanation: Most of the action takes place in her village and during the bus journey.
